name: grounding
description: Contracts over the provenance atom, so a rule can ask whether a number is safe to act on: untrusted(path, tol) counts the params that are not grounded (or grounded too loosely); untrusted_via(path, rel, tol) asks it of whatever a node links to — the reality a design is fitted against; depends_untrusted(path, tol) follows derived_from lineage, because a computed value is only as good as its inputs. Grounding is the substrate's one fixed epistemic verb; the provenance *label* stays a domain's own word.

vocabulary.description: Convention pack, not a node kind: every Quantity already carries `grounded` and `tolerance`. These contracts only read them — count what a branch, its links, or its lineage rests on that is not an observation.

solvers.name: grounding/untrusted
solvers.description: Counts the params at or under a path that are not safe to act on — not grounded, or grounded looser than the tolerance asked for. The direct question: is anything in this branch a guess? A rule wants `== 0`; a diagnostic reads the count.

params_doc.path: branch to inspect, itself and everything under it (default: the root)
params_doc.tolerance: the loosest grounding that still counts as safe; omit to ask only whether a value is grounded at all, ignoring how tight

solvers.name: grounding/untrusted_via
solvers.description: The same question asked of the reality a node is fitted against: follows one hop of a named link and counts the untrusted params in what it points to. This is the gate a design passes before it is acted on — the link says what the design depends on, this says whether those dependencies are observations or guesses.

params_doc.path: the node whose link is followed
params_doc.rel: the link name to follow one hop (e.g. 'fits_against', 'admits')
params_doc.tolerance: the loosest grounding that still counts as safe; omit to ask only about grounding, not tightness

solvers.name: grounding/depends_untrusted
solvers.description: Follows `derived_from` lineage transitively and counts the upstream params that are untrusted. A derived value is never grounded on its own — it is exactly as good as its inputs, and this is what prices that: a computed number resting on a guess is a guess wearing a number's clothes. A diamond in the lineage counts once.

params_doc.path: branch whose params' lineage is followed (default: the root)
params_doc.tolerance: the loosest grounding that still counts as safe upstream; omit to ask only about grounding
